differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with Lupus Nephritis and secondary hypercoagulability
Membranous Nephropathy with secondary bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Infective endocarditis with septic emboli to kidneys and lungs
Nephrotic syndrome (various causes) complicated by thromboembolic events
next_steps: Perform comprehensive autoimmune workup: ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3, C4), and antiphospholipid panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2-glycoprotein I)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation, considering the risk of hemorrhage versus the high burden of venous thromboembolism
Perform echocardiogram to rule out cardiac vegetations or intracardiac thrombus
Obtain renal biopsy to determine the etiology of nephrotic syndrome once clinically stable
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood and sputum culture results
Consult nephrology and rheumatology for specialized management
